briefing.headline: Rig up a Deep Diving Crankbait — finesse fall approach for muddy water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Deep Diving Crankbait is a strong choice for fall with muddy water and post frontal conditions.

Robertson: When the swimbait bite slows and fish scatter, pick up a deep-diving crankbait like the SPRO Little John DD 70 to put the majority of your weight in the boat. It produced the bulk of a 32-pound bag.

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

primaryApproach.retrieve: Robertson: When the swimbait bite slows and fish scatter, pick up a deep-diving crankbait like the SPRO Little John DD 70 to put the majority of your weight in the boat. It produced the bulk of a 32-p
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on creek channel, flat, point. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: Use 14-pound fluorocarbon to get the DT-20 crankbait down in current when fishing ledge fish
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on creek channel, flat, point. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: Wheeler: In fall, fish school up in ditches, on little flats in 2-4 feet, and especially around grass; on grassless lakes, key on shallow ditches with stumps that let bass ambush shad.

KVD: 'Grassy lakes have 3 depth zones: Shallow flats (0-4ft) — topwater, spinnerbaits, weedless plastics for sight-feeding fish. Weed edges (4-8ft) — the sweet spot, bass ambush from the edge, use soft plastics, jigs, creature baits. Deeper transi...

Robertson: When following postspawn bass down ditches with a deep diver, start by casting ahead and up on top, then bring the crankbait down the side of the ditch at about a 45-degree angle. The crankbait must hit bottom.

briefing.depthStrategy: Post-frontal fall fish pull tighter to cover and deeper structure at 8-18ft. The shad migration pauses but doesn't stop. Focus on the deepest fish in a given creek arm — channel bends and main creek points.
adjustIf: If skies cloud up and wind picks up, re-introduce moving baits — the reprieve from high pressure activates fish
If you find a cleaner pocket or tributary, switch to natural presentations — fish in cleaner water see more lures
If fish are short-striking, add a long pause after deflections to let the lure suspend
